extra.auc_note: deletion/insertion curves measured on the TRUE VCS by re-running the real ROM with top-SmoothGrad causes occluded (deletion) / restored (insertion); every point is a genuine emulator re-run. NaN = a genuinely flat experiment.

harness_positive_control.interpretation: corr=1 & precision@k=1 (on a non-degenerate column) ⇒ the scoring harness rewards a faithful map; the SmoothGrad numbers are then a true measurement.

extra.output_note: CONTENT output: RAM byte 89 read one-hot through soft_ram_peek (∂y/∂ram[89]=1) — the most causally-active candidate concept byte the oracle found at this state. The STE gradient is alive, so SmoothGrad concentrates on this TRUE causal byte (precision@1=1.0). With NO IG (x−baseline) factor, SG is alive even when the byte equals the IG baseline. Smoothing leaves the one-hot mass on the byte (it denoises an already-concentrated map): Δcorr vs vanilla = 0.0.
extra.scales_to_cluster_via: tools/cluster/xai_*.sbatch (E0-3) — batched SOFT-STE SmoothGrad over outputs×causes×games on GPU; the forward is bit-exact to this map.
extra.smoothgrad_sigma_units: raw RAM-byte units (byte∈0..255; σ=8.0 ≈ 3.1% of full scale)
extra.substrate: jutari (Julia) — Zygote SmoothGrad over the RAM tape + TRUE-VCS deletion/insertion re-runs (real-ROM, bit-exact oracle machinery).

vanilla_gradient_reference.interpretation: Δpearson = SmoothGrad − vanilla |grad|. ≈0 ⇒ smoothing did NOT change FAITHFULNESS (it denoises the visual map; on a one-hot content read the map is already on the true cause, nothing to denoise; on the vanishing position output both are 0 — smoothing cannot create a gradient).
